items.title: Emotions and Societal Changes: The Nature and Social Functions of “Neo-Emotions”
items.author: Agneta H. Fischer1University of Amsterdam, Amsterdam, the Netherlands
items.content: <article>Emotion Review, Ahead of Print. Neo-emotions are defined by one shared characteristic: they are a response to macro-societal changes. To gain full insight into when neo-emotions occur, a sociological approach needs to be complemented with a psychological perspective to conceptualize the ...</article>

items.title: Feeling Some Type of Way: Black Feeling and Emotional Ambiguity

items.content: <article>Emotion Review, Ahead of Print. items.title: Empathic Perception: A Neutral Framework for Understanding Empathy
items.author: Rosemary Elizabeth Kirkpatrick, Stephen Jonathan Whitty, Anita Louise Wheeldon
items.content: <article>Emotion Review, Ahead of Print. This paper reconceptualises evaluative processes pertinent to empathy as empathic perception: an evaluative, dynamic, and embodied process that provides morally neutral information as a perception to guide action and manage relationships.
